label: Article topic
helper: State the main subject in one clear sentence.
why: Defines the article’s central subject.

label: Primary goal
helper: Select the single most important outcome.
why: Changes the article’s emphasis, depth, and conversion logic.

label: Article format
helper: Select the structure that best fits the topic.
why: Determines the platform-native structure of the article.

label: Target audience
placeholder: Who will read this, and what matters to them?
helper: Describe the intended readers, context, and main needs.
why: Controls relevance, vocabulary, examples, and explanation depth.

label: Reader outcome
placeholder: What should readers understand, decide, or do afterward?
helper: Describe the practical result the article should create.
why: Keeps the article focused on a useful reader result.

label: Required key points
placeholder: List the facts, arguments, steps, or messages that must appear.
helper: Add the non-negotiable information the article must cover.
why: Prevents essential information from being omitted.

label: Source notes or brief
placeholder: Paste approved facts, research notes, product details, or source excerpts.
helper: Use this for factual material the model should rely on.
why: Provides a reliable factual base without creating many separate fields.

label: Output language
helper: The complete package will be written in this language.
why: Sets the language and its natural writing conventions.

label: Custom language
helper: Shown only when Other is selected.
why: Supports additional languages without expanding the primary select.

label: Article length
helper: Auto chooses the shortest length that fully serves the topic.
why: Controls content depth while discouraging unnecessary output.

label: Custom word count
helper: Use a realistic target for the available facts.
why: Allows precise length control only when explicitly requested.

label: Tone
helper: Use a broad tone here; detailed voice guidance is optional below.
why: Provides a fast and understandable style choice.

label: Brand or publisher
placeholder: Brand, publication, company, or author name
helper: Used for positioning, attribution, and suitable CTA wording.
why: Anchors the article to the correct publishing identity.

label: Working title
placeholder: Optional draft title or headline direction
helper: The model may improve it rather than preserve it exactly.
why: Captures an existing editorial direction without making it mandatory.

label: Reader knowledge level
helper: Controls explanation depth and terminology.
why: Prevents the article from being too basic or too technical.

label: Content angle
helper: Select the main way the topic should be framed.
why: Shapes the article’s thesis without duplicating the format field.

label: Point of view
helper: Controls whether the article uses first, second, or third person.
why: Keeps pronouns and narrative voice consistent.

label: Brand voice guidance
placeholder: Describe the voice in a few traits and examples.
helper: Add preferences not covered by the general tone field.
why: Allows meaningful brand differentiation without a long questionnaire.

label: Narrative style
helper: Controls how the article develops its ideas.
why: Changes article flow while remaining understandable to non-specialists.

label: Reference examples
placeholder: Describe publications, articles, or styles you like.
helper: Use as direction only; do not paste complete copyrighted articles.
why: Helps match editorial expectations without requiring direct imitation.

label: What to avoid
placeholder: List unwanted topics, claims, phrases, or stylistic habits.
helper: Combine exclusions and prohibited phrasing in one place.
why: Prevents brand, editorial, and messaging conflicts.

label: Primary keyword
helper: Leave blank for a reader-first article without a fixed keyword.
why: Aligns the article and metadata with a primary search query.

label: Secondary keywords
placeholder: Add related phrases, one per line or comma-separated.
helper: Use only genuinely related terms; they will not be forced.
why: Supports topical coverage without adding multiple keyword fields.

label: Search intent
helper: Auto infers intent from the topic, goal, and keywords.
why: Changes how directly the article answers and guides the reader.

label: Target location or market
helper: Use only when location changes relevance, rules, or terminology.
why: Supports regional relevance without forcing geography on every article.

label: Internal links
placeholder: Paste relevant URLs with optional anchor or page notes.
helper: Only useful links will be included; no pages will be invented.
why: Connects the article to existing site content and conversion paths.

label: External-link policy
placeholder: Choose how sources may be linked
helper: The model will never invent external URLs.
why: Controls citation and outbound-link behavior safely.

label: SEO priority
helper: Reader value remains mandatory at every level.
why: Sets the balance between natural editorial flow and search coverage.

label: Snippet target
helper: A snippet-friendly passage is added only when useful.
why: Enables a specific search-answer format without forcing one.

label: Content sensitivity
helper: Adds stricter handling for current or high-stakes topics.
why: Activates appropriate caution and verification rules.

label: Source URLs or references
placeholder: Paste approved URLs or complete reference details.
helper: Only supplied sources may be cited as real references.
why: Supports attribution and claim review without fabricated citations.

label: Approved quotations
placeholder: Paste exact quotes with speaker and source details.
helper: Quotes will be used only when attribution is sufficient.
why: Prevents invented quotations and unsupported attribution.

label: Claims requiring support
placeholder: List claims that must be evidenced, qualified, or omitted.
helper: Useful for sensitive, comparative, or performance-related statements.
why: Creates an explicit evidence threshold for risky claims.

label: Relevant jurisdiction
placeholder: Country, state, province, or regulatory area
helper: Used only when laws, regulations, or public rules may differ.
why: Prevents high-stakes content from assuming universal rules.
display: Show when `contentSensitivity` is not `Standard` or `Time-sensitive or current`

label: Restrictions and disclosures
placeholder: Add disclosures, required wording, brand rules, or claim limits.
helper: Use one field for mandatory wording and restrictions.
why: Combines important compliance constraints without specialist field overload.

label: Privacy and confidentiality
placeholder: State what must be anonymized, generalized, or omitted.
helper: Do not paste sensitive data unless necessary and permitted.
why: Protects private or confidential information in branded and case-led content.

label: Headline options
helper: Choose how many meaningfully different titles to generate.
why: Controls a high-value output without generating multiple full articles.

label: Include FAQ
helper: Add only when genuine reader questions would improve the article.
why: Keeps FAQ content optional instead of adding it automatically.

label: FAQ question count
helper: Shown only when FAQ is enabled.
why: Controls FAQ scope without exposing unnecessary settings.

label: CTA goal
helper: Use one relevant CTA or choose None.
why: Aligns the article’s next step with the actual publishing goal.

label: CTA destination
helper: No destination URL will be invented when this is blank.
why: Provides the real destination required for a usable linked CTA.

label: Output formatting
helper: Select the format easiest to paste into your publishing workflow.
why: Makes the result implementation-ready for different CMS workflows.
